title: Output/failure surface REMAINDER — typed SchedulerFailureError + manual-drain wedge fix, terminal_error/failure-callback surfaces, reporting-only browser panic hook with honest recovery contract, ordered-output promoted to constructional contract, walls + CI 46-to-N + authored failure probe carrying the timer-strand confirm-or-kill rider
purpose:
The output-and-failure-surface pillar governed by the WPORT-7 section of `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:141-154` at `acf7c69` (Size M `:143`; frozen dependency line "WPORT-2." `:144` — see the DEPENDENCY NOTE in the task). The arc's SCOPE, verbatim (`:146`): "Provide an intentional browser output sink and a complete observable failure surface: route `io:*` output, define the treatment of `erlang:display/1`, expose process execution errors, surface scheduler/pump failures, and install a browser panic hook. The addendum's corrected classification is binding: current output is mixed, not uniformly inert." The SCOPE RULING, verbatim (`:148`): "Scope ruling (2026-07-17, WPORT-5 fold — Tom + Waffles, joint authority per the WPORT-4 Ruling 5 precedent): WPORT-5 absorbs the browser output sink, the `erlang:display/1` treatment, and process-execution-error exposure (its R2 items 4 and 7). WPORT-7's REMAINDER: the scheduler/pump failure surface, the browser panic hook, and ordered-output guarantees." THIS BRIEF IS THE REMAINDER ONLY — WPORT-5's absorbed items are never-re-land boundaries below. The arc's ACCEPTANCE SHAPE, verbatim (`:152`): "Browser tests capture ordered `io:*` and `display/1` output through an explicit sink rather than incidental stdout. A crashing process exposes its PID and structured `ExecError`; a scheduler-turn failure rejects or invokes a documented error channel; a panic reaches the configured panic surface. The wall fails if output disappears, if `display/1` bypasses the contract, if summaries expose only an errored PID, or if a pump/turn simply stops without an observable cause." The first two acceptance clauses' crashing-process/`display/1` halves are LANDED by WPORT-5 (ground pack §4: `take_exit_error`, reasons vocabulary, additive `reason` field, cfg-split `display/1` — already satisfied); what remains of the acceptance text is exactly the remainder: the documented, TYPED scheduler-turn error channel, the panic surface, and the ordered-output guarantee proof.

TIMER-STRAND RIDER ATTACHMENT (arc `:92`, board finding 2026-07-17, probe-first rider — Waffles the Terrible): "RULED: confirm-or-kill measurement rides the NEXT wasm-timer-touching brief (WPORT-7+; WPORT-6's loader touches no timers) in the WPORT-3 probe pattern — no behavior change without cert re-sign." WPORT-7 IS the next wasm-timer-touching brief by the ruling's own criterion (ground pack §8.4): the sole fallible drain operation is the deadline seam `self.deadline.reconcile(...)` (sole `?` in `perform_drain`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:697-698`), the drain-failure walls inject throwing timer primitives, and `deadline_callback_fired` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1160`) is inside the failure-surface scope. The rider rides this brief's probe doc as its second section (D12/D14) — measurement only, never behavior; the TimerWheel no-fix boundary below is binding.

DESIGN STANCE (decided at Artemis's 2026-07-18 outline, drafted here as decided text): close the failure surface's asymmetry and type it. The manual-drain wedge (ground pack §1, the headline defect — `run_manual_drain`'s Err arm never resets Draining nor latches, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:663-679` vs `execute_queued_turn`'s reset+latch at `:640-641`) is FIXED with symmetric routing. Scheduler-turn failures become one NAMED error class (`SchedulerFailureError`, the WPORT-6 async-rejection pattern) carried by the latch rejection and the manual-drain Err (the manual leg latching per the OQ-C ruling), with a non-consuming `terminal_error()` accessor and a push `register_failure_callback` for hosts not using `await_exit`. The panic surface gets a process-global reporting-only hook (hand-rolled `std::panic::set_hook` via existing js-sys, console.error default plus optional registered plain-JS callback, constructor-installed behind a `Once`) and an honest recovery contract: post-panic the instance is latched-bricked — construct a fresh `WasmVm`. Ordered output is promoted from incidental to contractual: flush moves before waiter resolution (constructional, not microtask luck), the flush runs inside the drain envelope (kills the re-entrancy hole; OQ-B ruled HOLD-DRAINING), first-throw switches the flush remainder to console (order never interleaves across channels), and walls pin cross-process/multi-turn/fallback order. Zero new wake-path call sites, zero timers, PUSH-ONLY law intact, threaded path byte-identical.

STALENESS NOTE, load-bearing for the fold: the arc's WPORT-7 boundary evidence (`:150`) is pinned at `d9de35e` and stale on four of five legs (ground pack headline: the no-op `write_to_io_sink` leg retired by WPORT-5; the `display/1` `println!` leg true only for the threaded cfg; the no-ExecError-accessor leg superseded by `take_exit_error`; the rAF pump deleted by `c9bb64a` at WPORT-2's land — zero production pump symbols survive). Only the panic-hook-absent leg remains accurate, and unlike WPORT-4 (`:100`) and WPORT-5 (`:121`) the section carries NO supersession note. The remainder gap is therefore "channel exists but untyped, undocumented, asymmetric" — not "no channel exists". The supersession fold duties are FOLD-TIME work in the verification block (D15), not build-worker scope.

PIN DISCIPLINE, binding: evidence pin `acf7c69` (current main — TQ16 decided at the outline: advance to current main; believed-state wants the explicit choice). The evidence ground is `wport7-ground-pack.md` (same artifacts directory as this draft; checker-corrected synthesis of seven tracer reports; its §10 disagreement table is authoritative over any tracer claim), whose own pin is `1037aba` with tree `acf7c69` — `git diff --stat 1037aba acf7c69` touches only `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/tests.rs` and `crates/beamr/src/telemetry/spans.rs`, zero overlap with any cited file, so every pack citation is byte-identical at both commits and citable at `acf7c69` unqualified. This draft's own re-verifications at `acf7c69` are marked '[re-verified at acf7c69]'. The build worker MUST re-verify the pin-shift set named in the verification block at its ACTUAL base commit before writing a line — believed state is not citable state.
task:
EVIDENCE PIN `acf7c69`, stated as binding: the ground for every claim below is `wport7-ground-pack.md` (same artifacts directory as this draft), evidence pin `1037aba` with tree `acf7c69`, sole delta two test-only files — all code citations byte-identical at both commits. Sixteen tear questions (pack §11) were carried to Artemis's outline; all sixteen carry dispositions below (D1-D16, numbered by the pack's §11 TQ numbers) and the three carved conditionals are now RULED — all sixteen are decided text.

TEAR RULINGS (2026-07-17, Waffles the Terrible — torn APPROVED at `f13a9b7`; decided text, folded): his byte pass confirmed the wedge at his own hands — `execute_queued_turn`'s Err arm sets Idle and latches (`:640-641`) while `run_manual_drain`'s Err arm (`:673-678`) flushes and returns with state stuck at Draining and no `fail()`, a silent permanent wedge quieter than the latch because `last_error` never sets — and verified the `:1160`/`:1295` latch-only legs (fail() with no per-call surface). RULING OQ-A: ADDITIVE, ruled at his seat — WPORT-2 Ruling 3's frozen text constrains the rejection SEMANTICS (errors reject the Promise), not the value's type; a typed `SchedulerFailureError` rejects exactly as before, so nothing frozen is superseded and the joint-authority pattern does not trigger (the pack §8.2 routing was the right caution with the wrong conclusion — the supersession analysis IS the ruling, and the answer is no-supersession). The OQ-D data-property-mechanics reuse is RATIFIED with it: that ratification called the shape house convention, and house conventions exist to be reused. Recorded for veto as always. RULING OQ-B: HOLD-DRAINING — state stays Draining through the flush; the re-entrancy hole dies constructionally (closed-by-construction over behavioral promise — the LP-CLIENT round-4 lesson on what behavioral promises are worth against adjacent doors). RULING OQ-C: LATCH — full symmetric routing, one converged channel; the wedge exists precisely because the two error paths diverged, and a fix leaving them asymmetric would treat the symptom. RATIFIED ALONGSIDE: the timer-strand probe rider hosted here per arc `:92`'s own criterion with the hard TimerWheel no-fix boundary; `depends_on` WPORT-5+6 truthful-and-stronger per the WPORT-6 precedent; D15's arc-supersession duties on this fold (4/5 boundary legs stale with no supersession note — the believed-state class).

D1 WEDGE — routing gap, FIX with symmetric routing, decided. `run_manual_drain`'s Err arm (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:663-679` — sets Draining at `:663`, flushes and returns with no reset, no `fail()`) gains exactly what `execute_queued_turn` has (`:640-641`): reset state to Idle, then `fail()` — AND still returns the Err to the caller (the manual path's existing per-call surfacing is kept). Both paths converge on latch + surface. Today's consequence chain, cited: after a manual-drain Err, `request_external_turn` permanently no-ops (`Queued|Draining => Ok(())` `:589`), every later `run_step` returns the minted string "arbiter is already draining" (`:654`), parked `await_exit` waiters never resolve, and `last_error` is UNSET — a failure mode quieter than the latch, and worse under SETTLED IDLE (pack §8.1: a wedged arbiter never re-evaluates `settled_idle`, so waiters neither reject NOR resolve 'idle'). This is the arc's own "stops without an observable cause" wall condition reborn inside the post-rAF arbiter. The WPORT-4 tear Ruling 6 comment (`:181-188`) covers send_message ONLY (pack §1) — this Err arm is a routing hole, not ruled text. The latch half of the fix is RULED — OQ-C: LATCH; both paths converge on latch + surface.

D2 TYPED CHANNEL — one named class, proposed as ADDITIVE, decided; OQ-A RULED ADDITIVE. `SchedulerFailureError`: `js_sys::Error` + `set_name("SchedulerFailureError")` + `"{leg}: {detail}"` message (the leg slug is the kind position; no separate kind vocabulary) + ONE `data` property holding a JSON string `{"leg":"queued"|"manual"|"deadline"|"promise"|"spawn_edge","phase":<reconcile|...>,"terminal":true}` — the WPORT-6 `ArtifactLoadError` async-operational pattern. The two-precedent house law picks it (pack §5, VERIFIED): `ConnectionEventProtocolError` = sync throw for caller protocol violations (refuse-before-mutate); `ArtifactLoadError` = async rejection whose machine context rides a ratified `data` property; a scheduler-turn failure through the latch/await_exit rejection is an async operational failure — ArtifactLoadError shape; the "already draining" refusal is sync caller misuse — ConnectionEventProtocolError territory, typed as such (D10b), never conflated. The class becomes: the latch's stored value (waiter rejections reject WITH it), the manual-drain Err, and the accessor's payload. WPORT-2 Ruling 3 still holds verbatim — "Wrapper/scheduler/arbiter errors reject the Promise." (pack §8.2) — the value is typed, the semantics unchanged; the additive claim and the OQ-D data-property-mechanics reuse are RULED (OQ-A: ADDITIVE + reuse RATIFIED, ruled at Waffles' seat — the frozen text constrains semantics, not the value's type, so the joint-authority pattern does not trigger; recorded for veto as always). All five fail() leg classes carry leg-tagged data across the six existing production call sites plus the new manual site (pack §1 inventory, checker-confirmed exact: `:441` spawn_edge — sole caller `spawn_actor` `:304`, error swallowed into latch; `:616`/`:641`/`:722` queued; `:1160` deadline; `:1295` promise; new manual site per D1).

D3 LATCH-ONLY OBSERVABILITY — both surfaces, decided. Non-consuming `terminal_error() -> JsValue` (null | the JSON string; house JSON-string convention) + one-shot `register_failure_callback(Function)` invoked at first `fail()` with the typed error (push, event-driven — a host polling the accessor would be the host's own antipattern; the VM adds NO timer). Callback invocation is borrow-free (after state writes, same discipline as sink flush). This covers the two latch-only legs invisible today to non-awaiting hosts: `deadline_callback_fired` (`:1160`) and async promise completion (`:1295`). Ground for the latch's current opacity, cited: `last_error` at exactly four lines — field `:548`, init None `:580`, `await_exit` read `:729`, write in `fail()` `:840` — no getter, no clear (pack §1).

D4 WEDGED-VS-SETTLED — the fix dissolves the state, decided. With D1 landed there is no wedge: the manual-failure path resets and surfaces (and, per the OQ-C ruling, latches so waiters reject), the settled-idle complement is restored (pack §8.1 — the exited/errored/idle tri-state and PENDING-while-armed are tear-ratified and preserved). NO new "wedged" API. Walls assert counter coherence across both failure paths (pack §8.8 freight: `CallbackCounters`/`DeadlineCounters` are "COUNTED and falsifiable, never derived"; `queued_now` not stuck, snapshots truthful post-failure; the failure surfaces must not perturb counters).

D5 PANIC HOOK — hand-rolled, constructor-installed, process-global, decided. No new dependency (js-sys 0.3.100 suffices; preserves the WPORT-6 no-new-dependencies boundary — `console_error_panic_hook` REJECTED; it is absent from Cargo.lock today, pack §2). Installed at `WasmVm::new` behind a process-global `Once` (constructor precedent `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:70`, `:80-84`; NO `#[wasm_bindgen(start)]` anywhere in the repo — keep it that way). The reporting channel is process-global BY NECESSITY (pack hazard 10: the hook must not borrow VM RefCells — the panic may hold any of them): a global slot holding an optional plain-JS callback + console.error always.

D6 CAPTURE ROUTING — console.error + optional registered callback; NOT the io_sink, decided. The hook cannot safely reach a per-VM buffer (hazard 10). Free-function export `register_panic_callback(Function)` (process-global slot; last-wins, documented). The panic wall asserts the callback fired with the payload (message + location string) BEFORE the trap.

D7 RECOVERY CONTRACT — document, never resurrect, decided. Ruled honesty, stated in the module doc + probe doc: "post-panic the instance is latched (borrowed RefCells, stuck Draining); every scheduler-touching call re-traps; construct a fresh WasmVm." Ground, cited: `perform_drain` holds `self.scheduler.borrow_mut().run_until_idle()` across the whole run (`:683`); Draining set at `:631`/`:663`; abort runs no Drop code, so the borrow flag never decrements and ArbiterState stays Draining (pack §2, toolchain semantics INFERRED — the probe carries the on-platform confirmation). The edge-worker README note moves in the SAME COMMIT per the WPORT-2 consumer-lockstep precedent (pack §8.3, hazard 7: module-level `preloadedVmPromise` is never invalidated — one latch means every subsequent request on that isolate 500s instantly, or under the wedge, hangs; recovery = new isolate/VM). NO pre-trap waiter rejection from the hook (the exact unsafe borrow).

D8 PANIC WALL — cfg(test) panicking BIF, sync-entry trap caught as JS exception, decided. Zero production change (`bif_registry.register` takes a plain fn pointer, pack §6; the in-crate cfg(test) mod already reaches private fields). One panic per test, instance-terminal, LAST in its test's lifecycle (pack hazard 12). The wall asserts: the registered panic callback fired with the payload; the JS-visible trap surfaced. The arbiter-callback (async entry) abort case goes to the PROBE, not CI — `#[should_panic]` under Node is unproven and stays out.

D9 ORDERING CONTRACT — promote to constructional, decided. (a) `resolve_waiters` moves AFTER `io_sink.flush` in the drain sequence (one-line reorder at the bytes: `resolve_waiters` inside `perform_drain` at `:713`, flush after at `:648`/`:664-679` — flush-before-continuation becomes construction, not microtask luck); (b) walls pin: cross-process interleave = slice order (two processes, interleaved writes), multi-turn FairnessYield ordering (writes stream per turn `:717-726`, order total across turns), flush-on-failed-drain (already constructional at `:648`/`:664-679`, unwalled today). Per-turn total FIFO across processes and streams is the STATED guarantee; the interleaving itself remains scheduler policy (documented, not contract). Ground: one scheduler-wide FIFO (`CooperativeSinkBuffer`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/io_sink.rs:32-34`, `:52-58`), synchronous push-only writes at the BIF, Out/Err totally ordered in the single FIFO (pack §3).

D10 FALLBACK + RE-ENTRANCY — rule both, decided; OQ-B RULED HOLD-DRAINING. (a) First-throw switches the REMAINDER of that flush to console (ONE split point per flush, order preserved within each channel, never interleaved — replaces the per-write fallback's unbounded splitting at `io_sink.rs:97-120`; hazard 13). (b) The flush runs INSIDE the drain envelope: state stays Draining until flush completes (kills hazard 14 — a sink callback calling `run_step` synchronously gets the existing "already draining" refusal, typed as caller misuse per the two-precedent law; no newer-before-older delivery is possible). `finish_drain`'s fairness re-queue is unaffected (`queue_turn` from Draining is the existing edge `:722`). The envelope is RULED — OQ-B: HOLD-DRAINING.

D11 IO:FORMAT — refusal wall only, decided. Pin the facility-absent badarg (`io:format/2` needs an `io_message_facility` that only the threaded `supervision_integration` installs; cooperative `native_services` leaves it None → `ok_or_else(badarg)` at `io_bifs.rs:215`, pack §3). H3 FORBIDS naive `IoMessageFacility` wiring; the IO-server stays recorded future work (pack §8.9 boundary, cited verbatim below). Node cross-stream console order (stdout vs stderr as separate OS streams, hazard 16): documented out-of-contract in the SINK MODULE DOC; hosts wanting one ordered stream register a callback.

D12 TIMER RIDER — WPORT-7 HOSTS IT, decided. By arc `:92`'s own criterion (quoted in the purpose): the sole fallible drain op is `deadline.reconcile`, the drain-failure walls inject throwing timer primitives, `deadline_callback_fired` is in scope — this brief is timer-touching. Probe-only (WPORT-3 pattern, measurement never behavior): the strand confirm-or-kill rides the WPORT-7 probe doc as its second section. BOUNDARY, binding: no TimerWheel cursor "fix", no wheel edits at all — cert-resign territory (the boarded line's own terms).

D13 RULING 6 — adjacent, not reopened, decided. The drain-failure walls exercise the deadline-reconcile path ONLY; send_message's stranded-edge doctrine (WPORT-4 tear Ruling 6, comment `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:181-188`) stays ratified as-is; the wall doc-comment cites Ruling 6 and states non-reopening. Transient-throw evidence, if ever observed, reopens per the ruling's own clause ("reopens this as its own finding") — recorded as a future-work register entry, not machinery here.

D14 BROWSER ACCEPTANCE — probe + OPEN pillar + bundling invitation, decided. `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-7-PROBE-FAILURE.md` AUTHORED-NOT-RUN (house pattern, pack §8.7: WPORT-3-PROBE-THROTTLE "Status: NOT RUN — UNVERIFIED-ON-PLATFORM"; WPORT-6-PROBE-FETCH "AUTHORED-NOT-RUN"): browser panic surfacing (window.onerror vs Worker), the async-entry abort case (`#[should_panic]`-under-Node feasibility), Node-vs-browser console ordering, AND the timer-strand confirm-or-kill section (D12). The brief's close note INVITES one bundled manual browser+Worker sitting for the three accumulated pillars (WPORT-3 deadline, WPORT-6 fetch, WPORT-7 failure/panic) on Tom's/Annabel's word — observations never CI acceptance; WPORT-9 keeps the permanent gate (arc `:180` names panic surfacing in its wall).

D15 ARC SUPERSESSION — the WPORT-7 fold lands it, decided; FOLD-TIME duties, not build-worker work (enumerated in the verification block): boundary-evidence re-pin (`:150` at `d9de35e` → current; 4/5 legs stale, pack headline), "pump/turn" acceptance vocabulary rewritten against the HostArbiter, supersession note per the WPORT-4 (`:100`) / WPORT-5 (`:121`) precedent. Same fold commit: refresh `BROWSER-BIF-PROFILE.md:109`'s `display/1` row ([re-verified at acf7c69]: the status cell still reads "SILENT NO-OP (violation)" while the note cell records the SHIPPED R2-item-4 wiring — hazard 25 confirmed real) and add the addendum successor-authority line (the sealed 197-row profile IS the corrected classification's successor; the arc `:146` "addendum" reference names no in-repo document, pack §8.11).

D16 PIN + DEPENDS_ON — decided. Evidence pin `acf7c69` (advance to current main; every pack citation byte-identical at both, stated in the purpose's pin note). `depends_on: ["WPORT-5","WPORT-6"]` — see the DEPENDENCY NOTE.

LAWS BLOCK (verbatim carriers): NO-POLLING binding text, verbatim from the arc: "NO-POLLING (Tom's ruling): event-loop-driven scheduling — run-to-idle, wake on message; a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is “check whether something changed” is a design error." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:21` at `acf7c69`). WAKE-PATH PROHIBITION with the closed inventory: zero new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call sites outside tests; the closed set is WPORT-6 R2's 16-site inventory at the +1 shift, quoted from the pack §7: "the wake-path closed set from WPORT-6 R2 has shifted exactly +1 at every one of 16 sites (production callers now :190, :252, :304, :326, :341, :427; def :431/:434; infallible :439; arbiter :586; late-fire :1158; promise :1293; five test-only) — membership unchanged; checker-confirmed line-for-line" ([re-verified at acf7c69]: the five test-only sites sit at :1810, :2051, :3098, :3470, :3479). PUSH-ONLY SINK LAW, verbatim from WPORT-5.json via pack §8.6: "the R2 sink is PUSH-ONLY: writes flush to the host sink synchronously at the write, no flush timer, no buffer-poll, ever" — plus the standing grep "no timer/interval call in the sink module"; deferred/coalesced flushes driven by anything timer-shaped are tear conditions. THREADED BYTE-IDENTITY RANK, quoted from arc `:115`: wiring services into shared interpreter code "would break the threaded byte-identity law, which outranks acceptance-text breadth" — any ordered-output change touching shared native code (bifs.rs display, io_bifs.rs, context/mod.rs) leaves the threaded path byte-identical even where the acceptance text appears to demand more (byte-identical markers: `bifs.rs:210`, `context/mod.rs:1484`, `BROWSER-BIF-PROFILE.md:109`; pack §8.5). NO-KNOB, WPORT-2 Ruling 2 verbatim via pack §8.10: "NO configuration surface: BEAMR_EDGE_MAX_STEPS dies in this brief and must not be reincarnated as an arbiter knob" — no retry counts, failure toggles, or configurable panic behavior; edge-worker contract docs move with any behavior change. BELIEVED-STATE RE-VERIFY at the build base (pin-shift table in the verification block: fail() legs and call sites, arbiter state sites, flush sites, CI 46 names, wake-path 16 sites, WPORT-6 rider pins `:729-731`/`:839-845`). Vesper's `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` claim (untouchable). No builds in temporary directories, ever. ZERO new `#[allow]`. No `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]` (the panicking BIF is cfg(test)). The 9-leg gate bar on the final head.

STOP CONDITIONS, binding on the build worker: (1) anywhere this spec is underdetermined at the bytes, STOP and return with alternatives — never improvise a contract; (2) any APPARENT need for a new wake-path call site (`request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` outside tests) is a STOP, not a workaround; (3) any change to existing export behavior beyond THIS BRIEF'S OWN RULED EDITS — the D1 wedge routing, the D9 flush reorder, the D10b flush envelope, and the D2/D3 typed-error VALUE changes (the latch's stored value, waiter rejection values, the fallible wake path's thrown values at send_message/spawn/call/cast/timer_fired, the manual-drain Err value, all becoming the typed `SchedulerFailureError` — values only, never signatures) — is a STOP (signatures, return shapes, success-path vocabulary, completion/summary vocabulary, and every WPORT-5-landed surface are otherwise byte-untouched); (4) anything touching TimerWheel internals (`crates/beamr/src/timer.rs`) is a STOP — cert-resign territory per arc `:92`.

DEPENDENCY NOTE: the arc's frozen dependency line is WPORT-2 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:144` at `acf7c69`). This brief declares `depends_on: ["WPORT-5","WPORT-6"]` — truthful and stronger, per the WPORT-6 precedent: WPORT-5 (CLOSED at `1ec8ec9`) landed the sink + `take_exit_error` substrate this remainder builds on and its fold carried the `:148` scope ruling that DEFINES this remainder; WPORT-6 landed the 46-name CI baseline this brief's arithmetic extends and is the source of the +1 wake-path pin shift this brief's inventory cites. WPORT-2 through WPORT-4 are landed and subsumed by those edges.

requirements.title: Typed failure surface — failure.rs file pair, SchedulerFailureError with leg-tagged data, the manual-drain wedge fix with symmetric routing, terminal_error() + register_failure_callback, the latch stores the typed value
requirements.spec:
NEW FILE PAIR under the 500-line law (the twice-established precedent: `connection_events.rs`, `artifact_loader.rs`, each with `#[path]` test modules whose names join expected_tests with module prefix — pack §6; lib.rs production region is ~1,318 tokei code lines vs the 400-line entrypoint cap, ~3.3x over, so new work NEVER lands in lib.rs beyond mod registration and the small in-place arbiter edits): `crates/beamr-wasm/src/failure.rs` + `crates/beamr-wasm/src/failure_tests.rs`, module registration in lib.rs only.

ERROR CLASS (D2): `SchedulerFailureError` — `js_sys::Error` with `set_name("SchedulerFailureError")` and message `"{leg}: {detail}"` — the leg slug IS the message's kind position; no separate kind vocabulary exists (one closed set, one wall) — byte-following the WPORT-6 `ArtifactLoadError` async-operational pattern (the house two-precedent law, pack §5). ONE `data` property holding a JSON string with the pinned schema `{"leg":"queued"|"manual"|"deadline"|"promise"|"spawn_edge","phase":<reconcile|...>,"terminal":true}` — closed leg-slug set, pinned by an R4 wall; `phase` names the failing operation (the deadline-reconcile seam is the only fallible drain op at the pin, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:697-698`); `terminal` is literal true (the latch is permanent by ruled design). Payload mechanics follow WPORT-6's OQ-D shape (reuse RATIFIED at the tear, OQ-A).

WEDGE FIX (D1, OQ-C RULED LATCH): `run_manual_drain`'s Err arm (`:663-679`) gains the symmetric routing `execute_queued_turn` has (`:640-641`): reset state to Idle, then `fail()` with the typed error (leg "manual") — AND still returns the Err (now the typed class) to the JS caller. Latch-on-manual-failure is RULED (OQ-C: LATCH).

TYPED LATCH: `fail()` stores the typed `SchedulerFailureError` as the latch value; waiter rejections reject WITH it; every fail() leg carries leg-tagged data — existing production call sites, quoted from pack §1 (checker-confirmed exact): ":441 (`schedule_external_edge_infallible`; sole caller `spawn_actor` :304 — error swallowed into latch), :616 (`queue_turn`, latch + Err), :641 (`execute_queued_turn`), :722 (`finish_drain` fairness re-queue), :1160 (`deadline_callback_fired`, latch-only, no JS caller), :1295 (async promise completion, latch-only)" — legs: :441 spawn_edge; :616/:641/:722 queued; :1160 deadline; :1295 promise; plus the new manual site. The fallible wake path (send_message :190, spawn :252, call :326, cast :341, timer_fired :427 via `schedule_external_edge` :431-437) both latches and throws the typed error to the JS caller.

OBSERVABILITY SURFACES (D3): non-consuming `terminal_error() -> JsValue` returning null or the JSON data string (house JSON-string convention); one-shot `register_failure_callback(Function)` invoked at first `fail()` with the typed error — push, event-driven, borrow-free invocation after state writes (the sink-flush discipline). No polling surface, no clear/reset API (the latch is permanent; no-knob law).

WPORT-2 RULING 3 PRESERVED VERBATIM (OQ-A RULED ADDITIVE): "Wrapper/scheduler/arbiter errors reject the Promise." — the rejection SEMANTICS are unchanged; only the rejected VALUE becomes typed. The exited/errored/idle tri-state and PENDING-while-armed under SETTLED IDLE (pack §8.1) are tear-ratified and untouched.
acceptance: `crates/beamr-wasm/src/failure.rs` + `failure_tests.rs` exist under the 500-code-line law; lib.rs takes only mod registration plus the in-place arbiter edits (wedge routing, typed fail() legs, the two new surfaces' wiring)

`SchedulerFailureError` carries `set_name("SchedulerFailureError")`, a `"{leg}: {detail}"` message, and ONE `data` property holding the pinned JSON schema; the leg-slug set {queued, manual, deadline, promise, spawn_edge} is closed and pinned by a wall

After a manual-drain failure: state is Idle (not Draining), the Err returned to the caller is the typed class, and — per the OQ-C LATCH ruling — the latch holds the same typed value and parked waiters reject with it; after a queued-turn failure the identical latch + typed rejection holds (symmetric routing proven by paired walls)

`terminal_error()` is non-consuming (returns null pre-failure, the JSON string post-failure, repeatable); `register_failure_callback` fires exactly once at first `fail()` with the typed error, invoked borrow-free after state writes; the two latch-only legs (`deadline_callback_fired`, promise completion) are observable through both surfaces

WPORT-2 Ruling 3 semantics unchanged: errors still reject the Promise; `await_exit`'s tri-state and PENDING-while-armed behavior are byte-preserved; counter snapshots stay truthful across both failure paths (queued_now not stuck)
No retry/toggle/config surface of any kind (no-knob law); no new wake-path call sites; zero timers

requirements.title: Browser panic hook — process-global Once-installed set_hook, register_panic_callback, console.error default, honest recovery contract + edge-worker README same-commit
requirements.spec:
IN THE SAME failure.rs PAIR (R1's file set): the panic surface.

HOOK (D5): hand-rolled `std::panic::set_hook` via existing js-sys — NO new dependency (`console_error_panic_hook` rejected; absent from Cargo.lock at the pin, pack §2). Installed at `WasmVm::new` behind a process-global `Once` (constructor precedent `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:70`, `:80-84`; static-state once-guard precedent `:1373-1377`); NO `#[wasm_bindgen(start)]` (appears nowhere in the repo — keep it that way). The hook body: format message + location string, console.error ALWAYS, then invoke the optionally-registered plain-JS callback from the process-global slot. The hook MUST NOT touch VM RefCells (hazard 10 — the panic may hold any of them), MUST NOT reject waiters pre-trap (the exact unsafe borrow), MUST NOT attempt catch_unwind or resume — reporting-only.

REGISTRATION (D6): free-function export `register_panic_callback(Function)` — process-global slot, last-wins (documented in the export's doc comment).

RECOVERY CONTRACT (D7), documented in the failure.rs module doc AND the probe doc, verbatim contract text: "post-panic the instance is latched (borrowed RefCells, stuck Draining); every scheduler-touching call re-traps; construct a fresh WasmVm." The arbiter's error machinery never observes a panic (`fail()` runs only on JsValue Err returns — `last_error` stays None, waiters hang; pack §2) — the doc states this honestly rather than pretending the latch covers it. EDGE-WORKER README, SAME COMMIT (WPORT-2 consumer-lockstep precedent): `examples/edge-worker/README.md` gains the note that the cached module-level `preloadedVmPromise` serves a bricked VM after a panic (or latch) — recovery = new isolate/VM (pack §8.3, hazard 7).
acceptance: The hook installs exactly once per process across any number of `WasmVm` constructions (Once-guarded, walled); construction order and multiplicity do not double-install or leak callbacks

On panic the registered callback receives message + location BEFORE the trap, and console.error fires regardless of registration; with no callback registered, console.error alone fires (default proven)
The hook body contains zero VM-state access — no RefCell borrows, no waiter rejection, no counter writes (grep + review provable); no catch_unwind anywhere in the diff

The recovery contract text appears in the failure.rs module doc and the probe doc; `examples/edge-worker/README.md` carries the bricked-VM/fresh-isolate note in the same commit; `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker` passes without stub edits
The dependency tree is unchanged (no console_error_panic_hook, no web-sys, no new crates); no `#[wasm_bindgen(start)]` token appears in the diff

requirements.title: Ordered-output promotion — flush-before-waiters reorder, drain-envelope flush, first-throw-switches fallback, io:format refusal contract, sink module doc guarantee, io_sink.rs's first unit tests
requirements.spec:
REORDER (D9a): in the drain sequence, `io_sink.flush` completes BEFORE `resolve_waiters` runs, on both success and failure paths (the bytes today: `resolve_waiters` inside `perform_drain` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:713`, flush after at `:648`/`:664-679` — flush-before-waiter-continuation currently holds only via microtask luck, pack §3 incidental (a)). One-line-class reorder; flush-before-continuation becomes CONSTRUCTIONAL.

ENVELOPE (D10b, OQ-B RULED HOLD-DRAINING): the flush runs INSIDE the drain envelope — state stays Draining until flush completes (today state is already Idle when flush runs, `:719`, so a sink callback calling `vm.run_step()` synchronously nests a drain and delivers newer-before-older; hazard 14). With the envelope held, the re-entrant call gets the existing "already draining" refusal, typed as caller misuse (ConnectionEventProtocolError territory per the two-precedent law — sync refusal, distinct from `SchedulerFailureError`). `finish_drain`'s fairness re-queue is unaffected (`queue_turn` from Draining is the existing edge).

FALLBACK (D10a): first callback throw switches the REMAINDER of that flush to console — ONE split point per flush, order preserved within each channel, never interleaved across channels (replaces the per-write fallback's unbounded splitting at `io_sink.rs:97-120`; hazard 13). Absent-console behavior unchanged (silent drop, swallowed-by-design inventory, pack §1).

IO:FORMAT (D11): the facility-absent badarg is the pinned CONTRACT (`io_bifs.rs:215` `ok_or_else(badarg)`; only the threaded `supervision_integration` installs the facility, `:643`; cooperative `native_services` leaves it None, `wasm.rs:792-811`) — refusal wall in R4; NO IoMessageFacility construction in the wasm closure (H3 FORBIDS naive wiring; IO-server is recorded future work, pack §8.9).

MODULE DOC: `io_sink.rs`'s module doc states the promoted guarantee — per-write delivery granularity; total FIFO order within a turn across all processes and both streams; same-turn synchronous delivery including failed drains; flush-before-waiter-resolution; the one-split-point fallback rule; cross-process interleaving faithfully preserved but scheduler policy, not contract; Node cross-stream (stdout vs stderr) order OUT-OF-CONTRACT — hosts wanting one ordered stream register a callback.

FIRST UNIT TESTS: `io_sink.rs` gets its first unit tests (zero exist at the pin, pack §3) — buffer FIFO across tags, lossy-UTF-8, the split-point fallback logic — in-file cfg(test) or the house `#[path]` pattern, joining the CI arithmetic.

BOUNDARIES BINDING THIS REQUIREMENT: PUSH-ONLY law verbatim (LAWS BLOCK; standing greps ride R4); threaded byte-identity RANK — the shared native files (`bifs.rs`, `io_bifs.rs`, `context/mod.rs`) are NOT touched by this requirement at all (every edit is wasm-side: lib.rs drain sequence + io_sink.rs), so the byte-identical markers (`bifs.rs:210`, `context/mod.rs:1484`) hold trivially; H2/H3/H6 profile laws.
acceptance: Flush-before-waiter-resolution is constructional: a wall proves waiter continuations observe all of the turn's sink output already delivered, on both success and failure paths

Per the OQ-B HOLD-DRAINING ruling: state remains Draining across the flush; a sink callback re-entering `run_step` mid-flush receives the typed already-draining refusal and NO newer-before-older delivery occurs (walled); fairness re-queue from Draining still works

A callback that throws mid-flush produces exactly one split point: all pre-throw writes via the callback, all remaining writes via console, order preserved within each channel (walled); per-write alternation is gone from the bytes
`io:format/2` facility-absent badarg is pinned by a refusal wall; zero IoMessageFacility construction in the wasm closure (grep)
The io_sink module doc states the full promoted guarantee including the Node cross-stream out-of-contract note; io_sink.rs has unit tests where it had none

`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs`, `io_bifs.rs`, and `context/mod.rs` are byte-untouched by this diff (threaded byte-identity trivially held); PUSH-ONLY greps stay green — zero timer/interval tokens in the sink module

requirements.title: Walls + CI 46-to-N same-commit + fail-firsts + the authored-not-run failure probe with the timer-strand rider
requirements.spec:
WALLS (exact names drafter-proposed at build, WPORT-6 pattern — ratified or amended at the tear; each joins the pinned battery): (1) drain-failure QUEUED leg — throwing-setTimeout/clearTimeout double installed pre-construction (`HostPrimitives::probe` captures globals at construction, `:465-467`; swap/restore precedent `:2233-2268`; NO production seam needed, pack §6), typed rejection observed via `await_exit`, counters coherent; (2) drain-failure MANUAL leg — `run_step` Err is the typed class + state Idle + latch/waiter behavior per the OQ-C LATCH ruling; (3) `terminal_error()` surface (null pre-failure, JSON string post, non-consuming); (4) `register_failure_callback` one-shot push with the typed error; (5) leg-slug closed-set enumeration; (6) PANIC WALL (D8) — cfg(test) panicking BIF via fn-pointer registration (zero production change), sync-entry trap caught as JS exception, registered panic callback fired with payload BEFORE the trap, instance-terminal, one panic per test, LAST in its test's lifecycle, with a doc-comment citing WPORT-4 tear Ruling 6 and stating non-reopening (D13); (7) ordering: cross-process interleave = slice order; (8) ordering: multi-turn FairnessYield, order total across turns; (9) fallback one-split-point; (10) re-entrancy refusal per the OQ-B HOLD-DRAINING ruling; (11) `io:format/2` refusal; (12) flush-on-failed-drain (already constructional, unwalled today — pack §3); (13+) the io_sink unit walls (R3). Paired-symmetry framing: walls 1 and 2 are the D1 symmetry proof.

CI (WPORT-6 D11 law inherited): `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` extends the exact-name list AND the exact-count summary from 46 to the final N in the SAME COMMIT as the tests, delta-states-cause in the commit message. THREE edit sites, all named (pack hazard 20, [re-verified at acf7c69]): the `expected_tests` name list (`:42-87`), the exact summary grep "test result: ok. 46 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out" (`:96-101`), and the hardcoded PROSE "missing exact forty-six-test success summary" — the 'forty-six' prose line moves with the count. Missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` must still fail mechanically.

FAIL-FIRSTS, recorded-and-reverted per house law, minimum FOUR: (a) revert the wedge fix (Err arm loses reset+fail) — the manual-leg wall goes red; (b) drop one leg-slug/data-tag mapping arm — the enumeration wall goes red; (c) revert the flush reorder — the flush-before-waiters ordering wall goes red; (d) feed one stale expected name to the workflow assertion — the job fails despite passing test output. Run every new pin ten consecutive isolated times.

PROBE DOC (D12/D14): author `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-7-PROBE-FAILURE.md` — AUTHORED, NOT RUN (WPORT-3/WPORT-6 probe precedent; run gated on Tom's/Annabel's word; observations never CI acceptance). Section 1, failure/panic: browser panic surfacing (window.onerror vs Worker `onerror`), the arbiter-callback async-entry abort case (`#[should_panic]`-under-Node feasibility — the reason that case is NOT a CI wall), Node-vs-browser console ordering, post-panic latched-brick confirmation (the D7 contract's INFERRED toolchain legs). Section 2, TIMER-STRAND CONFIRM-OR-KILL (arc `:92` rider): the mid-turn 0ms-arm strand hypothesis (behind-cursor insert, unified one-shot re-arming an overdue deadline, ~1s macrotask churn) — measurement plan, observables, kill criteria; NO behavior change without cert re-sign; NO TimerWheel edits from this brief under any observation. CLOSE NOTE: invitation for ONE bundled manual browser+Worker sitting covering the three accumulated OPEN pillars — WPORT-3 deadline, WPORT-6 fetch, WPORT-7 failure/panic — sequencing unowned by this brief; WPORT-9 keeps the permanent gate.
acceptance: All new walls pass under the pinned wasm-bindgen Node runner from true idle, ten consecutive isolated runs each; no wall asserts timing precision; the panic wall is one-panic-per-test, instance-terminal, last in its lifecycle

The queued-leg and manual-leg drain-failure walls prove symmetric routing: same typed class, same latch semantics (per the OQ-C LATCH ruling), coherent counters on both paths; the throwing-primitive double needs zero production seams

`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` names every final wasm test exactly and asserts the exact final summary count in the same commit as the tests, with all THREE edit sites moved (name list, summary count, 'forty-six' prose) and delta-states-cause in the commit message; the 46-name baseline remains green and unweakened
Four fail-first mutations are performed, captured in the handoff, and reverted

`docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-7-PROBE-FAILURE.md` exists, authored-not-run, with the failure/panic section, the timer-strand confirm-or-kill section on the arc :92 rider's terms, and the three-pillar bundling invitation; nothing in CI depends on it; no TimerWheel edit exists anywhere in the diff
The wall doc-comment on the drain-failure walls cites WPORT-4 tear Ruling 6 and states it is exercised-adjacent, not reopened

boundaries: NEVER RE-LAND (the :148 scope ruling's absorbed items — WPORT-5's landed list, quoted from ground pack §4): "ungated IoSink/IoStream/NullSink vocabulary (`beamr/src/io_sink.rs:23-54`); retired no-op with one real `write_to_io_sink` body (+tagged); io:* + gleam sink routing with Err tags; scheduler holds/injects the sink (wasm.rs:159, :224-226, :808); `CooperativeSinkBuffer`/`HostIoSinkBridge` with OQ2-ruled console default and `register_io_sink` export; flush at end of every host turn including failed turns; cfg-split display/1; retained ExecError store with `take_exit_error`/`exit_error` peek (wasm.rs:128, :720-749); reasons.rs stable JSON vocabulary covering all 20 ExecError variants (:23-71); `WasmVm::take_exit_error` export + ADDITIVE `reason` field on errored completions with walling test (lib.rs:147-153, :741-752, :803-817, :848-873, test :4138-4233)". Every one of these surfaces is byte-untouched except the three ruled edits (wedge routing, flush reorder, flush envelope).

TIMERWHEEL NO-FIX (D12): no TimerWheel cursor "fix", no edits to `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs` at all — cert-resign territory per the arc :92 boarded line's own terms; the rider is measurement-only in the probe doc. Any wheel observation from the probe routes back to the board, never into this diff.

RULING 6 NON-REOPEN (D13): send_message's stranded-edge doctrine (WPORT-4 tear Ruling 6, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:181-188`) stays ratified as-is; the drain-failure walls exercise the deadline-reconcile path only and their doc-comment says so; transient-throw evidence reopens per the ruling's own clause as its own future finding.

WAKE-PATH PROHIBITION inherited verbatim: no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site outside tests; the 16-site closed inventory at the +1 shift (LAWS BLOCK quote) is the set the final diff must match; an apparent need for a new site is a STOP. NO-POLLING binds throughout — the failure surface, the hook, and the sink gain zero timers.

PUSH-ONLY SINK LAW verbatim (WPORT-5.json via pack §8.6): "the R2 sink is PUSH-ONLY: writes flush to the host sink synchronously at the write, no flush timer, no buffer-poll, ever"; the standing grep "no timer/interval call in the sink module" rides the verification bar; anything timer-shaped in a flush remedy is a tear condition.

THREADED BYTE-IDENTITY RANK (arc :115): the threaded path stays byte-identical even where acceptance text appears to demand more; `crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs`, `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs`, and `crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs` are untouched by this brief (byte-identical markers bifs.rs:210, context/mod.rs:1484).

NO-KNOB (WPORT-2 Ruling 2, pack §8.10): no retry counts, failure toggles, configurable panic behavior, or any configuration surface on the failure/panic/ordering machinery; the latch has no clear/reset API.

PANIC HOOK IS REPORTING-ONLY: no catch_unwind, no resume, no pre-trap waiter rejection, zero VM-RefCell access from the hook (hazard 10); no new dependencies (console_error_panic_hook stays out); no `#[wasm_bindgen(start)]`; the process-global callback slot is last-wins and documented.

RECORDED-FUTURE-WORK REGISTER boundaries (pack §8.9) — cite, do not absorb: ServiceUnavailable/notsup vocabulary push-down (H2/P1); the IO-server brief for io:format/2 (H3 FORBIDDEN naive wiring + standing grep "no IoMessageFacility construction in the wasm closure"); capability-brief preconditions (H1); the timer:sleep/1 reroute (P7).

LOADER FAIL-LATCH EXCLUSION RIDER untouched: the WPORT-6 rider (WPORT-6.json :88; its pins drifted to lib.rs:729-731/:839-845 at this pin) stands — loader failures never route through the latch; nothing in this brief changes `artifact_loader.rs`.

SETTLED-IDLE CONTRACT preserved (pack §8.1): the exited/errored/idle tri-state, PENDING-while-armed, and mass-resolve-on-settle are tear-ratified; the failure channel is built as settled-idle's COMPLEMENT, never a modification of it.

Untouchable: `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` (Vesper's claim). 500-code-line law on both new files. ZERO new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]` (the panicking BIF is cfg(test)-gated). No builds in temporary directories, ever.
verification:
BUILD-LEG ORIENTATION carries Tom's standing rule verbatim: "NO builds in temporary directories, ever." Worktrees go under the repo (`<repo>/.worktrees/<name>`, `.worktrees/` in `.git/info/exclude` — already added for beamr), every build sets `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=<repo>/target`, logs go to the artifacts dir.

FIRST ACTION at the build head — the PIN-SHIFT RE-VERIFY (believed state is not citable state; every citation in this brief is at `acf7c69` and main may have moved): re-verify at the ACTUAL base commit and record the re-verified line numbers in the handoff: (1) the fail() call-site inventory and its five leg classes (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:441, :616, :641, :722, :1160, :1295`; `last_error` at `:548`/`:580`/`:729`/`:840`); (2) the arbiter state sites (`:589` no-op arm, `:631`/`:663` Draining set, `:640` Idle reset, `:654` already-draining string, `:663-679` manual Err arm, `:713` resolve_waiters, `:719` Idle-before-flush, `:722` fairness re-queue); (3) the flush sites (`:637-648`, `:664-679`, `:717-726`) and the deadline seam (`:697-698`); (4) the CI 46-name baseline (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:42-87` names, `:90-95` loop, `:96-101` summary + 'forty-six' prose); (5) the wake-path 16-site inventory at the +1 shift (production :190/:252/:304/:326/:341/:427, def :431/:434, infallible :439, arbiter :586, late-fire :1158, promise :1293, test-only :1810/:2051/:3098/:3470/:3479); (6) the WPORT-6 loader-rider pins at their drifted lines (`:729-731`/`:839-845`). Any drift: re-derive, restate in the handoff, and proceed only if the design premises hold — else STOP condition 1 applies.

THE 9-LEG GATE BAR on the final head (as WPORT-6's brief carries it): (1) `cargo fmt --check`; (2)+(3) fresh-lint both clippy legs per the amended gate law — `cargo clean -p <crate>` before `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, Compiling/Checking line captured beside the exit code; cached greens stamped "checksum-verified cache hit", never "fresh re-lint"; (4) `cargo test --workspace --all-features --lib`; (5) `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; (6) `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker` (must pass WITHOUT stub edits; the README note is doc-only); (7) `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked`; (8) `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked`; (9) the pinned Node leg `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`, requiring the UPDATED workflow assertion to find every exact name and the exact final summary count (46 + the new pins), with missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` still failing — verified against the actual run output, not the yml alone.

PROHIBITION GREPS ride the bar: the final diff adds no `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site outside tests (inventory match against the 16-site closed set); the PUSH-ONLY standing greps — "no timer/interval call in the sink module" and zero `setTimeout`/`setInterval`/timer tokens in `io_sink.rs`; zero IoMessageFacility construction in the wasm closure; zero `catch_unwind`; zero `console_error_panic_hook`/`web_sys` tokens; zero `#[wasm_bindgen(start)]`; zero new `#[allow]`; zero non-test `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`; `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs`, `crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs`, `io_bifs.rs`, `context/mod.rs`, `artifact_loader.rs`, and `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` untouched.

Run every new pin in isolation ten consecutive times. Perform, record, and REVERT the four R4 fail-first mutations.

FOLD-TIME DUTIES (D15 — the arc-supersession fold; NOT build-worker work, landed by the folder at close): (1) re-pin the arc WPORT-7 boundary evidence from `d9de35e` to current with a supersession note per the WPORT-4 (:100) / WPORT-5 (:121) precedent — the historical pins stay unrewritten, cite-the-brief-not-the-lines; (2) rewrite the "pump/turn" acceptance vocabulary against the HostArbiter (zero production pump symbols survive since `c9bb64a`); (3) refresh `BROWSER-BIF-PROFILE.md:109`'s `display/1` row status cell ([re-verified at acf7c69]: still "SILENT NO-OP (violation)" while the note cell records the SHIPPED wiring — hazard 25 real); (4) add the addendum successor-authority line: the sealed 197-row profile IS the corrected classification's successor (the arc :146 "addendum" reference names no in-repo document, pack §8.11).

Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose`, `cargo --version`, `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version`, `node --version`, wasm target, UTC timestamps, exact commands, the exact final test inventory, fail-first outputs, and the pin-shift re-verify table. The handoff CARRIES: the `SchedulerFailureError` data schema and closed leg-slug set; the recovery-contract text as landed; the promoted ordering guarantee as documented; per-wall counter snapshots (pack §8.8 freight); the probe doc path with its authored-not-run status, the timer-strand section's terms, and the three-pillar bundling invitation; and the arc-board lines this brief touches — the timer-strand rider hosted (arc :92 terms), the WPORT-3 deadline pillar and WPORT-6 fetch pillar still OPEN, terms unchanged.
